pattern: Building the result on a stack lets each new character trigger an in-place check for a completed match, collapsing repeated removals into one pass.
hints: Repeatedly deleting the leftmost match is equivalent to processing characters left to right and removing a match as soon as its last character lands.
Push characters onto a stack; after each push, check whether the stack now ends with part.
When the top of the stack matches part, pop those characters off, then continue; join the stack at the end.

edge_inputs.description: removal creates a new adjacent match
entire string is the part repeated

stress.description: long string over a small alphabet with a short part
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(n))
    plen = rng.randrange(1, 4)
    part = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(plen))
    return [s, part]
